testFiles.description: Unit tests for the health gate module that orchestrates shader and geometry validation into a single pass/fail checkpoint.

tests.name: gate passes when shaders compile cleanly and all geometry attributes are valid
tests.description: Create a mock renderer, scene, camera, and geometry systems with valid attributes. ShaderErrorCollector has no errors after compile. Verify runStartupHealthGate returns { passed: true, shaderErrors: [], geometryErrors: [] }.
mocks: BufferGeometry with all REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES valid per system

tests.name: gate fails and returns structured shader errors when compilation produces errors
tests.description: Configure ShaderErrorCollector to report errors after renderer.compile(). Verify gate returns passed: false with shaderErrors populated with shaderType and message fields.
assertions: each shaderError has shaderType ('VERTEX' or 'FRAGMENT') and message string
mocks: ShaderErrorCollector seeded with VERTEX shader error

tests.name: gate fails and returns structured geometry errors when attributes are missing
tests.description: Provide geometry systems where one system's BufferGeometry is missing a required attribute (e.g., 'position'). Verify gate returns passed: false with geometryErrors listing the missing attribute and a descriptive reason.
assertions: geometryErrors includes entry with attribute: 'position' and reason containing 'missing'

tests.name: gate fails when geometry attribute has wrong itemSize
tests.description: Provide geometry with 'position' attribute having itemSize 2 instead of 3. Verify gate detects the mismatch.
assertions: geometryErrors includes entry with attribute: 'position' and reason containing 'itemSize'

tests.name: gate fails when geometry attribute contains NaN values
tests.description: Provide geometry with valid attributes but inject NaN into the position array. Verify gate detects non-finite values.
assertions: geometryErrors includes entry with reason containing 'NaN'

tests.name: gate fails and aggregates both shader and geometry errors when both are present
tests.description: Configure both shader compilation errors and geometry validation errors. Verify the result contains errors from both categories.

tests.name: gate logs structured failure with [EAVI health-gate] prefix on shader error
tests.description: Spy on console.error. Trigger a shader compilation failure. Verify console.error is called with a message containing '[EAVI health-gate]' and the shader error details.
assertions: console.error message includes shader type information

tests.name: gate logs structured failure with [EAVI health-gate] prefix on geometry error
tests.description: Spy on console.error. Trigger a geometry validation failure. Verify console.error is called with a message containing '[EAVI health-gate]' and the geometry error details.
assertions: console.error message includes attribute name and reason

tests.name: gate does not log to console.error when all checks pass
tests.description: Spy on console.error. Run gate with all valid inputs. Verify console.error is not called.

tests.name: gate calls renderer.compile(scene, camera) to force synchronous shader compilation
tests.description: Verify that runStartupHealthGate calls renderer.compile with the provided scene and camera arguments.

tests.name: gate validates all geometry systems from ModeManager, not just the active one
tests.description: Provide a ModeManager-like object with multiple geometry systems (PointCloud, ParticleField, RibbonField). Make one non-active system have invalid geometry. Verify the gate catches the error even though it's not the active system.
mocks: Multiple geometry systems, one with invalid attributes

tests.name: gate includes systemName in geometry errors when available
tests.description: Verify that geometry errors include an optional systemName field to identify which geometry system failed.
assertions: geometryErrors entry has systemName field identifying the failing system
mocks: Named geometry systems with one failing

tests.name: gate does not throw — returns result object on failure
tests.description: Trigger both shader and geometry failures. Verify the function returns normally (no throw) with a result object. This is critical: the gate must be non-throwing so the render loop can stay alive showing the placeholder.
assertions: returns HealthGateResult object with passed: false

tests.name: HealthGateResult type has correct shape
tests.description: Verify the returned result conforms to the HealthGateResult interface: { passed: boolean, shaderErrors: Array<{ shaderType: string; message: string }>, geometryErrors: Array<{ attribute: string; reason: string; systemName?: string }> }.
assertions: shaderErrors entries have shaderType and message
geometryErrors entries have attribute and reason

testFiles.description: Integration tests verifying the render loop respects the health gate result, blocking steady-state entry on failure and proceeding on success.

tests.name: render loop enters steady-state and removes placeholder when health gate passes
tests.description: Set up a full render loop with valid geometry systems and no shader errors. Run several frames. Verify placeholder mesh is removed from scene and geometry system's draw() is called.
assertions: geometry system draw() called on subsequent frames

tests.name: render loop stays on placeholder and does not enter steady-state when health gate fails due to shader errors
tests.description: Set up render loop with a ShaderErrorCollector that reports errors after compile. Run several frames. Verify placeholder mesh remains in scene and geometry system's draw() is never called.

tests.name: render loop stays on placeholder when health gate fails due to geometry errors
tests.description: Set up render loop with geometry systems that have invalid attributes. Run several frames. Verify placeholder persists and draw() is not invoked.

tests.name: console.error is called with structured failure info when gate blocks steady-state
tests.description: Set up render loop where health gate will fail. Spy on console.error. Run frames. Verify console.error was called with [EAVI health-gate] prefix and error details during the initialization phase.
assertions: console.error called with message containing '[EAVI health-gate]'
error details include specific failure reason

tests.name: render loop does not throw when health gate fails — loop stays alive for debugging
tests.description: Previously, validateShaderCompilation() threw on failure. Verify the new health gate integration does NOT throw, keeping the render loop alive so developers can read the console. The loop should continue scheduling rAF callbacks.
assertions: requestAnimationFrame continues to be called after gate failure

tests.name: health gate is invoked before first geometry draw call
tests.description: Instrument the order of calls: health gate check must happen before any geometry system draw(). Even on a passing gate, verify the temporal ordering is correct.
assertions: health gate validation completes before first draw() call
mocks: Call-order tracking spies on compile, validate, draw

tests.name: health gate replaces the old throwing validateShaderCompilation behavior
tests.description: Verify that the render loop no longer calls validateShaderCompilation() directly (which throws). Instead it should use the health gate which is non-throwing. This confirms the migration from the old pattern.
assertions: validateShaderCompilation is not called directly by render loop
shader errors result in gate failure, not thrown exception

testStrategy.approach: Unit tests for healthGate.ts mock the WebGL renderer, ShaderErrorCollector, and BufferGeometry to simulate all pass/fail combinations. Integration tests for the render loop verify that the gate result controls the steady-state transition. Existing tests for shaderValidation.ts and geometryValidator.ts remain unchanged — healthGate composes them.
testStrategy.mockingPattern: Follow existing test conventions: THREE.WebGLRenderer as mocked class, vi.spyOn(window, 'requestAnimationFrame') for frame simulation, vi.fn() for geometry system methods, real ShaderErrorCollector instances with mocked GL/shader objects.
coverageGoals: All four acceptance criteria mapped to specific tests
Both shader-only and geometry-only failure paths
Non-throwing behavior (critical behavioral change from old pattern)
